summary: README.md needs full rewrite
description: README.md is significantly out of date. Says six tutorial chapters complete (there are 13), roadmap tables show kernel primitives and fc.py features as not-started when most are done, references getting-started/ directory which is actually docs/, and the status section doesn't reflect current project state.

summary: forth/kernel/README.md primitives table incomplete
description: The primitives table stops at the early set (DUP, DROP, SWAP, OVER, +, -, @, !, EMIT, CR, HALT). Missing all primitives added for chapters 6-12: DO, LOOP, I, *, /MOD, KEY, 0BRANCH, BRANCH, =, <>, <, >, 0=, AT. Memory layout also shows an old START address.

summary: forth/tools/README.md shows completed features as not-yet
description: The 'What fc.py does not do' table still lists IF/THEN/LOOP as not yet implemented — they are done. The compilation pipeline description omits DO/LOOP, IF/ELSE/THEN, BEGIN/AGAIN/UNTIL. Uses CLOADM which should be LOADM.

summary: Bare Naked kernel: replace POLCAT with direct PIA0 keyboard scan
description: CODE_KEY (line 350 of forth/kernel/kernel.asm) is the kernel's only ROM dependency, calling JSR [$A000] (POLCAT via Extended Color BASIC ROM). Replacing this with direct PIA0 access makes the kernel fully ROM-independent and ready for cartridge delivery without BASIC loaded. Work includes: (1) new KBD-SCAN primitive writing col_mask to PIA0-B ($FF02) and reading PIA0-A ($FF00); (2) rewritten CODE_KEY with register-based debounce and MATRIX2ASCII lookup table; (3) AND and OR primitives; (4) KEY-HELD? Forth word; (5) optional KEY? non-blocking variant. Also requires fc.py word table updates for kbd-scan, and, or.

summary: AND / OR bitwise primitives
description: Add CODE_AND ( n1 n2 -- n ) and CODE_OR ( n1 n2 -- n ) to kernel.asm (CFA table entries + implementations). Add 'and' and 'or' to fc.py kernel_words dict. These are needed by KEY-HELD? and general bitwise application code.

summary: PIA0-A direction register init in START
description: Add bare-metal PIA0 initialisation to the START routine in kernel.asm: write to PIA0-A control register ($FF01) to set port A as input and enable the data register, and write to PIA0-B control register ($FF03) to set port B as output. BASIC does this at boot; a bare-metal cartridge must do it explicitly. Prerequisite for KBD-SCAN and the new CODE_KEY.

summary: KBD-SCAN primitive ( col_mask -- row_bits )
description: Add CODE_KBD_SCAN to kernel.asm: write col_mask to PIA0-B ($FF02), read PIA0-A ($FF00), invert, mask bit 7 (joystick comparator), push result. Passing $00 strobes all columns simultaneously for a fast any-key check. Add 'kbd-scan' to fc.py kernel_words. Depends on issue 6 (PIA0 init).

summary: MATRIX2ASCII table + CODE_KEY rewrite
description: Replace CODE_KEY's JSR [$A000] / POLCAT call with a direct PIA0 scan loop. Includes: (1) register-based two-poll debounce (no RAM needed); (2) MATRIX2ASCII BSR subroutine with a 56-entry lookup table (7 cols x 8 rows) mapping matrix position to ASCII; (3) conditional assembly (IFDEF COCO3) for CoCo 3 variant table covering F1/F2/ALT. Eliminates the last ROM dependency. Depends on issues 6 and 7.

summary: Add INCLUDE directive to fc.py
description: Implement INCLUDE <filename> as a compile-time host directive in fc.py. When the tokeniser encounters INCLUDE, it reads the named file (relative to the source file's directory) and splices its tokens into the stream before continuing. Enables a lib/ directory of shared Forth source. Standard ANS Forth FILE word set behaviour, implemented as a cross-compiler host operation.

summary: KEY-HELD? word and KB-Cx constants in lib/keyboard.fs
description: Create forth/lib/keyboard.fs containing: column mask constants (KB-C0 through KB-C6) as Forth CONSTANT definitions, and KEY-HELD? ( col_mask row_bit -- flag ) defined in pure Forth as: SWAP KBD-SCAN AND. Depends on issues 5 (AND), 7 (KBD-SCAN), and 9 (INCLUDE). Applications include it with: INCLUDE lib/keyboard.fs

summary: KEY? non-blocking variant ( -- char|0 )
description: Add CODE_KEY_NB to kernel.asm: same PIA0 scan as CODE_KEY but returns 0 immediately if no key is pressed rather than spinning. Useful for game loops, timeouts, and polling. Add 'key?' to fc.py kernel_words. Depends on issue 8 (MATRIX2ASCII table, which KEY? will share via BSR).

summary: Typewriter test app in src/typewriter
description: Create src/typewriter/typewriter.fs: a simple app that calls KEY in a loop and EMITs each character to the screen. Tests the new PIA0-based KEY implementation end-to-end. ENTER advances to the next row (CR), CLEAR resets cursor to top-left. Includes cursor display, arrow key movement, backspace, SHIFT support, and VSYNC-based auto-repeat.

summary: Demo: Snake game
description: Classic snake game using VDG semigraphics-4 color blocks. Arrow keys control direction, snake grows when eating food, game over on wall/self collision. Exercises keyboard input, game loop timing (VSYNC), screen drawing, and game state management.

summary: Demo: Drawing program
description: Arrow keys move a brush cursor, number keys select colors. Uses VDG semigraphics modes for a 64x32 color pixel canvas. Demonstrates semigraphics block characters, color selection, and interactive screen painting.

summary: Demo: Sound/music player
description: Explore the CoCo's 6-bit DAC on PIA1. Simple tone generator or melody player. Could play a sequence of notes or allow interactive keyboard-to-tone mapping (piano). Opens up PIA1 hardware access.

summary: Demo: Conway's Game of Life
description: Cellular automaton on the VDG semigraphics grid. Visually satisfying and a good stress test for Forth performance on the 6809. Could use semigraphics-4 for color or text mode for simplicity.

summary: Demo: Starfield
description: Stars flying toward the viewer, simulating depth with different speeds. Pure VDG semigraphics, no math library needed. VSYNC-timed animation loop.

summary: Demo: Clock / stopwatch
description: Big digit display using block characters, ticks off the VSYNC 60Hz counter. Could include a stopwatch mode with start/stop/reset. Demonstrates timed display updates and large character rendering.

summary: Demo: Maze generator and navigator
description: Random recursive backtracker draws maze walls in real time using semigraphics, then lets the player navigate with arrow keys. Requires a random number generator (could seed from VSYNC timing or keyboard).

summary: Demo: Text adventure
description: Navigate between rooms with N/S/E/W commands. Room descriptions, items to pick up, simple puzzles. Pushes the Forth toolchain into string handling, data tables for room connections, and state tracking. A CoCo classic.

summary: Demo: Sprite animation
description: Walking character or bouncing ball using VDG semigraphics. Explores smooth movement, erase-draw cycles, and VSYNC-timed animation on the CoCo's character-mapped display.

summary: Tool: Memory monitor / hex editor
description: Hex dump display, live memory editor, peek/poke any address. Arrow keys navigate, hex input to modify bytes. Actually useful as a development tool for inspecting kernel state, variables, and screen RAM.

summary: Demo: Reaction time game
description: Wait for a random delay, flash the screen, measure how fast the player hits a key. Uses VSYNC frame counter for timing. Simple but fun, and demonstrates precise timing measurement.

summary: Demo: Serial terminal
description: Hook up the CoCo's serial port (PIA1 bit-banged or cartridge slot UART) and make a dumb terminal. Most ambitious hardware-wise — connects the CoCo to the outside world. Practical for DriveWire or other serial communication.

summary: Tool: 6809 disassembler
description: Disassemble memory regions into 6809 mnemonics. Takes a start address and length, displays address, hex bytes, and decoded instruction. Must handle variable-length instructions (1-5 bytes), $10/$11 prefix pages, and indexed addressing postbyte decoding. Big but highly useful for inspecting compiled Forth output and kernel code on-device.

summary: Tool: Stack viewer
description: Display data stack and return stack contents on screen. Show stack depth and values in hex. Small enough to be a library word — no kernel changes needed. Immediately useful for debugging any Forth program. Could be invoked with a word like .S (print stack).

summary: Tool: Word lister
description: Show all compiled words with their CFA addresses. Helps verify that fc.py produced what you expected. Could display kernel primitives and app-defined words with their entry points. Requires fc.py to emit a word table or symbol list into the binary.

summary: Tool: Breakpoint trap
description: A Forth word (e.g. BRK or DEBUG) that can be inserted anywhere in Forth code. When executed, dumps registers and stack contents to the screen and waits for a keypress before continuing. Like a printf-debugger for Forth. Implementable as a library word using existing primitives.

summary: Tool: Memory compare
description: Diff two memory regions byte-by-byte, highlighting differences. Takes two addresses and a length. Useful for verifying binaries match expectations or checking if RAM has been corrupted. Display shows address, expected byte, actual byte for each mismatch.

summary: Tool: Execution tracer
description: Single-step through the Forth thread, showing each word as it executes with stack state. The most powerful debugging tool but also the hardest — requires hooking DOCOL and NEXT in the kernel to intercept each threaded dispatch. Could show CFA address, word name (if symbol table available), and stack contents at each step.

summary: Lib: Hi-res graphics (PMODE)
description: Closed: VDG mode switching exists in vdg.fs (set-sam-v, set-sam-f, set-pia, reset-text). No unified pmode abstraction needed — separate per-mode libraries (rg-pixel.fs for RG6, future cg-pixel.fs for CG modes) conserve app space by only loading what each app uses.

summary: Lib: Pixel plot and line drawing
description: Closed: Pixel plot and line drawing exist in rg-pixel.fs as optimized CODE words (rg-pset, rg-pget, rg-line, rg-hline). Future CG-mode equivalents would go in a separate cg-pixel.fs library.

summary: Lib: Circle drawing
description: CIRCLE ( cx cy r color -- ) draws a circle using the midpoint circle algorithm. Optional filled variant. Extended Color BASIC's CIRCLE also supports aspect ratio and arc segments — consider a simplified subset. Depends on issue 32 (pixel/line drawing).

summary: Lib: Flood fill (PAINT)
description: PAINT ( x y fill-color border-color -- ) flood-fills a bounded region. Extended Color BASIC uses a scanline seed fill. On the 6809 with limited RAM, a simple stack-based fill may hit memory limits — consider a scanline approach. Depends on issue 32.

summary: Lib: DRAW turtle graphics
description: A DRAW-like command inspired by Extended Color BASIC's DRAW string language. Compact command strings like 'U10R5D10L5' to draw shapes. Commands: U/D/L/R (up/down/left/right), E/F/G/H (diagonals), M (move to), B (blank move), N (no cursor update), A (angle 0-3), S (scale), C (color). Extremely efficient for defining complex shapes in minimal code. Implement as a Forth word that interprets a command string stored in memory.

summary: Lib: Sound and DAC access
description: SOUND ( pitch duration -- ) generates a tone via the CoCo's 6-bit DAC on PIA1 ($FF20 bits 2-7). Pitch controls frequency by toggling the DAC output in a timed loop, duration controls length. Also expose DAC-OUT ( value -- ) for direct 6-bit DAC writes (values 0-63 shifted left 2 into $FF20). Foundation for all sound/music features. BLOCKED: DAC writes produce no audio after all-RAM mode (STA $FFDF) is enabled. Full investigation in src/sound/SOUND.md. forth/lib/sound.fs and src/sound/sound.fs exist but silent until the mode-switch/IRQ/PIA path is understood.

summary: Lib: PLAY music command
description: A PLAY-like command inspired by Extended Color BASIC's PLAY string language. Compact music notation like 'T5O3CDEFGAB' to play melodies. Commands: A-G (notes), O (octave 1-5), L (note length), T (tempo 1-31), P (pause), # (sharp). Extremely efficient for encoding music in minimal code. Uses the DAC (issue 36) for tone generation. Implement as a Forth word that interprets a music string stored in memory.

summary: Demo: Joy to the World (music + graphics)
description: Inspired by the Extended Color BASIC manual's sample program. Plays 'Joy to the World' melody while drawing colorful graphics on screen. Combines the PLAY music library (issue 37) with PMODE hi-res graphics (issue 31). A showcase of audio-visual capability.

summary: Demo: Animated rocket
description: Inspired by the Extended Color BASIC manual's animated rocket program. Uses GET/PUT-style sprite blitting to move a rocket shape across a hi-res graphics screen. Demonstrates sprite animation, erase-draw cycles, and PMODE graphics. Depends on issues 31-32 (graphics library).

summary: Demo: Hi-res font renderer
description: Inspired by the Extended Color BASIC manual's custom character program. Renders text characters in PMODE hi-res graphics using pixel-level font definitions. Shows how to display readable text on a graphics screen where the normal VDG text mode is unavailable. Depends on issues 31-32.

summary: Demo: Back to Bach (music player)
description: Inspired by the Extended Color BASIC manual's Bach Invention program. Plays a classical piece using the PLAY music library. Demonstrates complex musical notation, tempo changes, and sustained performance. A pure audio demo showcasing the DAC sound system. Depends on issue 37 (PLAY library).

summary: Demo: Electronic piano
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's electronic piano. Maps keyboard keys to musical tones — press A through K to play notes from middle C up one octave. Visual feedback shows which note is playing. A simple but satisfying interactive demo. Depends on issue 36 (sound library).

summary: Demo: Kaleidoscope
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's kaleidoscope program. Draws symmetric random patterns using semigraphics-4 SET/RESET. Four-way symmetry creates mesmerizing visual effects. Uses random colors and positions with VSYNC timing. A classic CoCo demo that works with existing semigraphics capabilities — no PMODE needed.

summary: Demo: Ready Aim Fire (shooting game)
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's Ready Aim Fire game. Move a cannon left/right along the bottom of the screen, fire at targets. Text-mode semigraphics with sound effects. Exercises keyboard input, animation, collision detection, and scoring.

summary: Demo: Typing test
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's typing test program. Displays a phrase, times the user as they type it, calculates words per minute. Uses VSYNC frame counter for accurate timing. Counts errors by comparing input character-by-character. A practical demo that exercises KEY?, screen output, and timing.

summary: Demo: Music composer
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's music composer program. Generates random melodies with user-selectable length and tempo. Uses random note selection from a scale, with color screen changes on each note. A generative art/music piece. Depends on issue 36 (sound library).

summary: Demo: Bouncing ball
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's bouncing dot program. A ball bounces around the screen, reflecting off walls. Uses semigraphics-4 SET/RESET for smooth-looking animation. Could evolve into a simple Pong or Breakout. VSYNC-timed for consistent speed.

summary: Demo: Dice/Craps game
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's dice and craps programs. Simulates rolling two dice with ASCII art dice faces on screen. Implements full craps rules: 7/11 on first roll wins, 2/3/12 loses, otherwise roll for point. Sound effects for rolls and results. Needs a random number generator (VSYNC-seeded).

summary: Demo: Speed reading trainer
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's speed reading program. Displays text one line at a time at a user-selected words-per-minute rate. Could use a stored passage (e.g. opening of a novel). Tests how fast you can read and comprehend. VSYNC-timed display updates.

summary: Demo: Math quiz (timed)
description: Inspired by the Color BASIC manual's arithmetic and 'Beat the Computer' programs. Generates random math problems (addition, subtraction, maybe multiplication). Player must answer before a timer runs out. Tracks score and percentage correct. Sound feedback for right/wrong answers. VSYNC timing for countdown display.

summary: VDG mode switching library (forth/lib/vdg.fs)
description: Reusable library for controlling the MC6847 VDG and SAM chip display modes. Words: set-sam-v ( v -- ) sets SAM V0-V2 display mode bits; set-sam-f ( offset -- ) sets SAM F0-F6 display offset; set-pia ( bits -- ) sets PIA $FF22 bits 7-3 preserving 2-0; reset-text ( -- ) restores default Alpha text mode. Encapsulates all the fiddly SAM/PIA register poking so graphics programs don't have to.

summary: 5x7 bitmap font library (forth/lib/font5x7.fs)
description: Reusable 5x7 pixel font for rendering text in graphics modes. 37 glyphs: space, A-Z, 0-9. 7 bytes per glyph with pixels in bits 7-3. Written to $6000 at init using fb pattern from Tetris. glyph-addr ( char -- addr ) maps ASCII to font data address. Reusable by any program needing text in non-Alpha VDG modes.

summary: Demo: VDG Mode Demo (src/vdg-modes/)
description: Cycles through all 11 MC6847 display modes: Alpha Internal, SG4, SG6, CG1-CG6, RG1-RG6. Mode table at $5800: 11 entries x 16 bytes with VRAM base, size, PIA/SAM values, mode name. Text modes write VDG chars directly; RG modes use font byte per row; CG modes nibble-expand font. Each mode shows a visual pattern (color bars, stripes, checkerboard) plus the mode name. Key press advances to next mode; loops forever. Depends on #51 (vdg.fs) and #52 (font5x7.fs).

summary: Demo: Digital Rain (Matrix effect)
description: Matrix-style falling green characters on black VDG text screen. 32 independent columns with random speeds, bright inverse-video heads and dim normal trails. Trail mutation and random spawning create an organic feel. SAM double-speed for fast updates. Uses rng.fs and screen.fs libraries.

summary: Demo: Tetris
description: Full SG4 semigraphics Tetris. 10x16 board, 7 standard pieces with rotation, gravity with speed-up, line clearing, scoring, next-piece preview, high score table. Zero-flicker draw-first-clean-stale rendering. Arrow keys move/rotate, space for hard drop. Key repeat with DAS timing. Uses rng.fs, screen.fs, print.fs, datawrite.fs libraries.

summary: Lib: RG6 artifact-color pixel primitives (rg-pixel.fs)
description: Pixel primitives for RG6 mode (PMODE 4) with NTSC artifact coloring. Treats pixel pairs as artifact-color units (00=black, 11=white, 01=blue, 10=red) at effective 128x192 resolution. Words: rg-pset ( x y color -- ) plot artifact pixel, rg-pget ( x y -- color ) read pixel, rg-pcls ( -- ) clear to black, rg-hline ( x1 x2 y color -- ) fast horizontal line. VRAM at $3000, 6144 bytes, 32 bytes/row. Foundation for all Space Warp graphics.

summary: Lib: Bresenham line drawing for RG6 (rg-pixel.fs)
description: Add rg-line ( x1 y1 x2 y2 color -- ) to rg-pixel.fs using Bresenham's algorithm. Draws lines in artifact color for maser beams, enemy fire, and tactical display borders. Must handle all octants correctly. Depends on #56.

summary: Lib: Software sprite library (sprite.fs)
description: Software sprites for RG6 artifact-color mode. Sprite data format: width, height, then row bytes (2bpp artifact-color packed). spr-draw ( addr x y -- ) draws sprite with color 0 as transparent. spr-erase ( addr x y -- ) erases sprite footprint to black. Background save/restore for clean overdraw without flicker. Used for Endever, Jovians, bases, stars, triton missile ball. Depends on #56.

summary: RG6 artifact graphics test app (src/rg-test/)
description: Test application for RG6 artifact-color graphics. Exercises pixel plot, line draw, sprite draw/erase, and trig-based line angles. Fixed spr-erase→spr-erase-box rename. All tests passing at various angles, sprite draw/erase, and text rendering. Visual verification via XRoar with NTSC artifact display. Depends on #56-58.

summary: Lib: Sine/cosine lookup table for angle-based firing
description: 8-bit sine/cosine lookup table for converting firing angles (0-360 degrees) to dx/dy vectors. 91-entry table (0-90 degrees) with quadrant mirroring. Fixed-point values scaled for tactical view dimensions. Used by masers and triton missiles for angle-to-direction conversion.

summary: Optimize graphics primitives as kernel assembly
description: Move rg-pset, rg-line (Bresenham), spr-draw, spr-erase-box, and rg-char to 6809 assembly kernel primitives. ITC Forth overhead makes these ~30x slower than native. rg-pset alone is called per-pixel by lines, sprites, and fonts. Also page out ROMs ($FFDE) to reclaim $8000-$FEFF as RAM. Expected gains: rg-line from ~1500 to ~40 cycles/pixel, spr-draw from ~800 to ~50 cycles/pixel.

summary: fc.py: tokenizer captures CODE...;CODE blocks as raw asm text
fc.py: parser builds code_definitions from CODE tokens
fc.py: preprocess_asm + assemble_code_words via lwasm
fc.py: compiler integrates CODE words into binary layout
CODE word end-to-end test app
Migrate 5 graphics primitives from kernel to library CODE words
description: Moved rg-pset, rg-line, spr-draw, spr-erase-box, rg-char from kernel.asm to CODE words in their respective libraries (rg-pixel.fs, sprite.fs, rg-text.fs). Freed ~652 bytes of kernel ROM. Scratch variables remain in kernel RAM at $0050+ for CODE word EQU resolution.

summary: Font-art.fs: digit 7 looks like letter T
description: The 7 glyph in font-art.fs used centered stem (.#. / $30) for all rows below the top bar, making it identical to T. Fixed by shifting top two stem rows right (..# / $0C) to create a diagonal descent.

summary: Move VRAM from $5000 to $5200 for more app space
description: App binary exceeded $5000 (12288 byte limit). Moved VRAM base to $5200, giving 12800 bytes for app code. All game data addresses shifted up by $200 ($6800->$6A00, sprites $6900->$6B00, etc). CODE word buffer addresses updated.

summary: Add UNLOOP kernel primitive
description: UNLOOP ( -- ) (R: loop-sys -- ) removes DO/LOOP control parameters (index and limit) from the return stack without branching. Needed for early exit from loops via EXIT. Without it, EXIT inside a DO/LOOP corrupts the return stack. Implementation: LEAS 4,S then NEXT. Add CFA_UNLOOP to kernel.asm and 'unloop' to fc.py kernel_words(). Standard ANS Forth word.

summary: Add ROT kernel primitive
description: ROT ( a b c -- b c a ) rotates top three stack items. Third most common stack operation after DUP and SWAP. Currently requires >R SWAP R> SWAP (4 words). Assembly: load 3 values from U, store back rotated, ~12 bytes.

summary: Add 2DUP kernel primitive
description: 2DUP ( a b -- a b a b ) duplicates top two stack items. Currently written as OVER OVER. Assembly: LDD ,U / LDY 2,U / STD ,--U / STY ,--U or similar, ~10 bytes.

summary: Add +! kernel primitive
description: +! ( n addr -- ) adds n to the cell at addr. Extremely common for incrementing variables. Currently requires DUP @ ROT + SWAP ! (5 words). Assembly: pop addr and n, load cell, add, store back, ~10 bytes. Applied at 19 sites in spacewarp.fs + 3 in library files.

summary: Add >R R> R@ kernel primitives
description: >R ( n -- ) (R: -- n ) moves TOS to return stack. R> ( -- n ) (R: n -- ) moves return stack TOS to data stack. R@ ( -- n ) (R: n -- n ) copies return stack TOS. 6 bytes each, 24 bytes total kernel cost.

summary: Add 2DROP kernel primitive
description: 2DROP ( a b -- ) drops top two stack items. Currently DROP DROP (2 words, 2 NEXT dispatches). Assembly: LEAU 4,U then NEXT, ~4 bytes. Applied at 9 sites in spacewarp.fs.

summary: Add PICK kernel primitive
description: PICK ( n -- x ) copies the nth stack item to TOS. 0 PICK = DUP, 1 PICK = OVER, etc. General-purpose deep stack access without return stack gymnastics. Assembly: LDD ,U (get n) / ASLB ROLA (n*2) / LDD D,U / STD ,U, ~8 bytes.

summary: Add INVERT kernel primitive
description: INVERT ( n -- ~n ) bitwise complement. Complement to AND/OR. Assembly: LDD ,U / COMA / COMB / STD ,U, ~6 bytes.

summary: Add XOR kernel primitive
description: XOR ( n1 n2 -- n3 ) bitwise exclusive or. Completes the bitwise trio with AND and OR. Assembly: LDD ,U++ / EORA ,U / EORB 1,U / STD ,U, ~8 bytes.

summary: Add MIN and MAX kernel primitives
description: MIN ( n1 n2 -- min ) and MAX ( n1 n2 -- max ). Moved from app-level CODE words to kernel. Saves per-app overhead.

summary: Add J kernel primitive
description: J ( -- n ) pushes the outer loop index in nested DO/LOOPs. The outer index is 4 bytes deep on the return stack (past the inner loop's index and limit). Assembly: LDD 4,S / STD ,--U, ~6 bytes.

summary: Add +LOOP kernel primitive
description: +LOOP ( n -- ) like LOOP but increments index by n instead of 1. Enables counting by arbitrary steps and counting down. Requires signed overflow detection for correct termination across the limit boundary. More complex than LOOP — ~20 bytes.

summary: Add ABS kernel primitive
description: ABS ( n -- |n| ) absolute value. Moved from Forth definition in rg-pixel.fs to kernel CODE word. Assembly: test sign, negate if negative.

summary: Add MOD and / kernel primitives
description: Not needed as separate primitives. /MOD gives both remainder and quotient; use /MOD SWAP DROP for division, /MOD DROP for modulus. Documented in kernel README.

summary: Relocate VRAM to $0600, contiguous app space
description: VRAM moved from $4000 to $0600 (below app). App is now a single contiguous block at $2000 (~24K via CLOADM). No more --hole flag needed. fc.py --hole kept for backward compat but unused.

summary: Relocate kernel to $E000 via all-RAM mode
description: Kernel moved from $1000 to $E000 (ROM 2 region). Bootstrap at $0E00 enables all-RAM (STA $FFDF — NOT $FFDE), copies staged kernel from $1000 to $E000. Stacks at $DE00/$E000. 24K runtime RAM at $8000-$DDFF. Previous attempt failed because $FFDE clears TY (keeps ROM), $FFDF sets TY (all-RAM). XRoar works fine with -ram 64.

summary: Interactive boot animation docs (boot-animation.html)
description: Step-by-step HTML animation showing the DECB load, bootstrap, all-RAM, copy, and START sequence. Shows memory map with VRAM at $0600, 24K loadable app, 24K runtime RAM, kernel at $E000. Navigate with arrow keys or buttons.

summary: Add -ram 64 to all demo Makefiles
description: All demos need -ram 64 for XRoar since kernel is at $E000 (requires SAM all-RAM mode with 64K DRAM). Updated: typewriter, kaleidoscope, tetris, rain, rg-test. spacewarp already had it.

summary: Fix rg-test: spr-erase renamed to spr-erase-box
description: rg-test.fs referenced spr-erase which was renamed to spr-erase-box in an earlier commit. Updated both references. Build and run verified.

summary: Kernel scratch variables for beam system (VAR_BEAM_*)
description: Add ~6 bytes of scratch variables to kernel.asm for the beam-trace/beam-draw-slice/beam-restore-slice CODE words: VAR_BEAM_BUF (2 bytes, path buffer pointer), VAR_BEAM_VRAM (2 bytes, VRAM address scratch), VAR_BEAM_CNT (2 bytes, pixel count scratch).

summary: Beam pixel-save library (forth/lib/beam.fs)
description: New library with three CODE words: beam-trace, beam-draw-slice, beam-restore-slice.

summary: Kernel: TYPE primitive ( addr len -- )
description: Add TYPE to kernel — loops over len bytes at addr calling EMIT for each. ~20 bytes at $E000. Enables string-based text output, saving app space vs char-by-char EMIT calls.

summary: Kernel: COUNT primitive ( c-addr -- addr+1 len )
description: Add COUNT to kernel — converts counted string (length byte + chars) to addr+len form. ~12 bytes at $E000.

summary: fc.py: S" and ." string literal support
description: Add S" ... " syntax to fc.py. Compiles inline string data in thread with BRANCH to skip over it, pushes addr+len at runtime. Add ." as shorthand for S" ... " TYPE. New slit IR node. Breakeven at 4+ chars vs CHAR-by-CHAR.

summary: Library: rg-type ( addr len -- ) for RG6 text output
description: Add rg-type to rg-text.fs — Forth word that loops over string calling rg-emit for each byte. Keeps RG6-specific code out of kernel. Enables S" ... " rg-type pattern.

summary: Move font data to kernel as raw FCB bytes
description: Moved 59 glyphs (472 bytes) from font-art.fs Forth literals into kernel.asm as DOVAR + FCB data. init-font becomes: font-data $9000 472 CMOVE. Eliminated fp variable, fb, f8, f0 helper words. Saved 1,538 bytes of app space (21529 -> 19991). Kernel cost: 474 bytes. Font loads in <1ms vs visible pause before.

summary: Move sprite data to kernel as raw FCB bytes
description: init-sprites in spacewarp.fs costs ~394 bytes of app space to push-and-store 60 bytes of sprite pixel data via Forth literals. Moved 5 sprites into kernel.asm as DOVAR + FCB data (62 bytes kernel). init-sprites becomes: sprite-data SPR-SHIP 60 CMOVE. Saved 380 bytes of app space (21909 -> 21529). Kernel cost: 62 bytes from 6.6K headroom.

summary: Add PROX-SCAN kernel primitive for spatial proximity queries
description: Generic kernel primitive: prox-scan ( cx cy radius array count -- bitmask ). Scans (x,y) byte pairs, returns bitmask of entries within Manhattan distance. ~70 bytes kernel code. Uses return stack for loop params, handles 8-bit overflow on distance sum.

summary: Resize missile sprites from 5x5 to 3x3 and fix animation rate
description: Shrink missile sprites from 5x5 to 3x3 in kernel sprite-data. Update SPR-MSL2 address (offset changes from 48 to 41), init-sprites copy count (60→46), and all missile centering offsets (2→1). Fix missile animation rate from every-frame to every-2-frames for visible alternation.

summary: Add MDIST kernel primitive and refactor call sites
description: MDIST ( addr1 addr2 -- d ) Manhattan distance between two (x,y) byte pairs. 7 call sites converted, 262 bytes saved. Remaining abs uses are bounding box checks or loose values.

summary: fc.py: inline CONSTANT values as LIT (selective)
description: fc.py inline_constants() inlines CONSTANT references as LIT values. Initially selective (<=3 refs, MAX_INLINE_REFS=3, saved 262 bytes). Then raised to inline all constants (#246). 23 low-ref + 22 high-ref constants inlined. Each ref: 80cy->31cy (49cy saved). App savings from selective phase: 262 bytes.

summary: fc.py: inline high-ref CONSTANTs when space available
description: MAX_INLINE_REFS raised to 999 (inline all). High-ref constants inlined: SHIP-POS(44 refs), JOV-POS(43), JOV-DMG(38), BASE-POS(16), BHOLE-POS(16), etc. Cost +324 bytes but saves ~49cy per reference across all paths. Combined with #245, all CONSTANT definitions eliminated from output.

summary: Bounce demo: HSYNC beam-chasing testbed with 4 balls
description: New demo at src/bounce/ — 4 bouncing balls in RG6 artifact-color mode with three rendering modes (0=free-running, 1=VSYNC only, 2=VSYNC+HSYNC beam-chasing). Time-sliced rendering (2 balls/frame, 30fps per ball). Font HUD shows blanking offset and mode. Up/down arrows adjust offset, 0/1/2 keys switch modes. Proved HSYNC beam-chasing technique before applying to Space Warp (#262). Includes README documenting findings and CoCo keyboard matrix reference.

summary: Kernel: vsync CODE word + wait-past-row blanking offset fix
description: Converted vsync from Forth (BEGIN/UNTIL polling loop) to CODE word for tighter detection. Fixed wait-past-row blanking offset from 35 to 70 HSYNC lines — 70 matches the actual NTSC vertical blanking interval (262 total lines - 192 visible). Both in forth/lib/screen.fs.

summary: Update docs/reference.html: fix keyboard matrix, add missing libraries
description: reference.html has the old wrong keyboard matrix (all arrows in C3). Fix to match corrected keyboard.fs and kernel KEY_TABLE. Add missing library sections: rg-pixel.fs, sprite.fs, beam.fs, trig.fs, sound.fs, dac.fs, screen.fs (wait-past-row, count-blanking). Update memory map for all-RAM configuration.

summary: Add cross-references and headers to markdown working notes
description: Add research/exploratory header to COCO_INTERRUPTS.md. Link USERGUIDE.md and MARKETING.md from spacewarp/README.md. Cross-reference SPEC.md and AI_DIVERSITY_STRATEGY.md. Trim out-of-scope hardware spec from COCO_RENOVATION.md.

summary: Screen capture MCP crash fix — use-after-free in non-ARC ObjC
description: Fixed crash in screen-capture-mcp C++ server. Root cause: SCShareableContent cache used raw ObjC pointers without ARC (project compiled without -fobjc-arc). Completion handler's autorelease pool could free SCWindow/SCDisplay objects while capture_window was iterating them. Fix: explicit CFRetain in completion handler, manual [content release] in each consumer function, serialized access with mutex. Removed 2-second cache (unnecessary for sequential MCP calls). Crash logs confirmed SIGSEGV in objc_msgSend from capture_window.

summary: Move screen CODE words to kernel (vsync, wait-past-row, count-blanking)
description: Move 3 screen sync primitives from forth/lib/screen.fs into kernel.asm. These are hardware-only (PIA $FF00-$FF03), no app dependencies. Add CFA symbols to kernel map so fc.py resolves them as kernel words. Remove CODE blocks from screen.fs. Est. ~82 bytes reclaimed from app space.

summary: Move rg-pixel CODE words to kernel (rg-pset, rg-line)
description: Move 2 RG6 pixel primitives from forth/lib/rg-pixel.fs into kernel.asm. Both use only kernel scratch vars (VAR_RGVRAM, VAR_LINE_*). General-purpose for any RG6 app. Est. ~429 bytes reclaimed from app space.

summary: Move sprite CODE words to kernel (spr-draw, spr-erase-box)
description: Move 2 sprite primitives from forth/lib/sprite.fs into kernel.asm. Both use only kernel scratch vars (VAR_SPR_*, VAR_RGVRAM). General-purpose for any RG6 sprite app. Est. ~369 bytes reclaimed from app space.

summary: Move rg-char CODE word to kernel
description: Move rg-char font rendering primitive from forth/lib/rg-text.fs into kernel.asm. Uses kernel config vars at $75-$7B (font base, glyph size, rows, bpr, row height). General-purpose for any text rendering. Est. ~75 bytes reclaimed from app space.

summary: Move beam CODE words to kernel (beam-trace, beam-draw-slice, beam-restore-slice, beam-find-obstacle, beam-scrub-pos)
description: Move 5 beam system primitives from forth/lib/beam.fs and spacewarp.fs into kernel.asm. All use kernel scratch vars (VAR_BEAM_*, VAR_LINE_*, VAR_RGVRAM). beam-find-obstacle and beam-scrub-pos are pure buffer operations. General-purpose for any beam/line-of-sight system. Est. ~779 bytes reclaimed from app space.

summary: Add KCODE directive to fc.py for kernel-space CODE words
description: New compiler directive KCODE...;KCODE that assembles CODE words into the kernel region ($E86A-$FEFF) instead of app space. Requires second origin counter in fc.py, kernel binary patching, and FVAR_* resolution for app-space variable references. Enables moving game-specific CODE words (~4185 bytes) out of app space. Phase 2 after library words are promoted to kernel primitives (#332-#336).

summary: Missile stuck at 0 velocity for headings >= 720
description: sin() normalization only subtracted 360 once, so angles >= 720 read garbage past the 91-byte SINTAB. Could produce dx=dy=0, freezing the missile in place. Fix: use /MOD 360 for proper modulo normalization.

summary: save-ship-bg to CODE word
description: save-ship-bg is a Forth wrapper (837cy) calling bg-save CODE (140cy). The Forth wrapper costs ~700cy in ITC overhead — CONSTANT lookups, C@ fetches, arithmetic. Converting to CODE saves ~500cy. Low risk, small effort.

summary: Redesign Endever sprite — chevron with twin red engines
description: Replaced boring triangle with shaped chevron: nacelle gaps, twin red engine pixels at stern. Blue hull, more character.

summary: Redesign starbase sprite — 7x7 ring with spokes
description: Expanded base from 5 to 7 rows. Blue ring with blue spokes, hollow center. Shifted QCOUNTS from $80B0 to $80B4 to avoid overlap with MSL2 sprite data.

summary: Add U< kernel primitive
description: Unsigned less-than comparison. Standard Forth word, useful for boundary checks across all apps. Added to kernel as primitive #75.

summary: Add 0MAX kernel primitive
description: Clamp to >= 0. Promoted from spacewarp Forth definition to kernel primitive #76. Removed Forth definition, 6 call sites now use kernel primitive. Saved 22 bytes in app.

summary: Add 2* kernel primitive
description: Arithmetic shift left by 1. Added as kernel primitive #77. Used in jov-pos-addr (2 * → 2*), saved 4 bytes.

summary: Add WITHIN kernel primitive
description: Range test: ( n lo hi -- flag ). Standard Forth WITHIN using unsigned trick (n-lo u< hi-lo). Added as kernel primitive #80.

summary: Add 2/ kernel primitive
description: Arithmetic shift right by 1. Added as kernel primitive #78. Replaced 9 occurrences of 1 RSHIFT in spacewarp, saved 36 bytes.

summary: Add 0MIN kernel primitive
description: Clamp to <= 0. Mirror of 0MAX (#372). Added as kernel primitive #79.

summary: Add KEY_SCAN kernel subroutine
description: Extracted keyboard scan logic from CODE_KEY_NB into a KEY_SCAN subroutine callable via BSR/LBSR from CODE words. Returns ASCII in A, 0 if no key. Preserves X, U. Enables CODE words to scan keyboard without duplicating PIA logic.

summary: Relocate kernel variables from $0050 to kernel space ($EF03+)
description: Moved 50 bytes of VAR_* variables from $0050 (BASIC direct page) to end of kernel at $EF03+. Variables are now copied to all-RAM with the rest of the kernel by the bootstrap. Eliminates DECB record at $0050, making binaries LOADM-safe from Disk BASIC. All VAR_* accesses were already extended addressing — no performance change.

summary: Replace hardcoded kernel variable addresses with KVAR-* constants
description: fc.py now injects KVAR-* Forth constants from kernel.map (e.g. KVAR-CUR, KVAR-RGVRAM, KVAR-RGFONT). Replaced all hardcoded $0050/$0057/$0075-$007B references in spacewarp.fs, bounce.fs, sprtest.fs, tetris.fs, rain.fs, typewriter.fs, and rg-pixel.fs with symbolic KVAR-* names. Addresses resolve automatically when kernel layout changes.

summary: Dynamic kernel staging address in fc.py
description: Staging address for the kernel copy now packs right after the bootstrap ($0E19) instead of hardcoded $1000. Reclaims ~480 bytes of headroom before APP_BASE. fc.py patches the bootstrap's LDX source address and LDX #APP_BASE automatically. Prevents staged kernel overflow when KCODE words are appended.

summary: Top-level Makefile with DSK disk image target
description: New top-level Makefile: 'make' builds kernel + all demos, 'make dsk' creates build/demos.dsk with all 7 programs via Toolshed decb, 'make clean' removes artifacts. CoCo 8.3 filenames: BOUNCE, KALEIDSC, RAIN, TETRIS, RG-TEST, TYPEWRTR, SPACEWRP. Checks for decb on PATH with helpful install message.

description: Define KERN_VERSION in kernel.asm (e.g. EQU $0100 for 1.0) so it appears in kernel.map. fc.py and downstream projects can read it. Print or embed version in hello world output.

Shows the practical flow (issues.jsonl tracking, measure-don't-estimate cycle counts, auto-launch XRoar for testing, kernel vs app trade-offs). Verify all library words across forth/lib/*.fs are present and accurate. Document the KCODE directive (kernel-space CODE words). Candidate names to verify: Jim Reed, Wayne Day, Cray Augsburg, Jerry Semones, Dan Keen. Verify dates and roles against Rainbow archives.

Covers online archives (colorcomputerarchive.com, archive.org), books (Tandy's Little Wonder by Swygert, 1993), community histories (Coco Chronicles), interviews (Boyle/Kalish, Phillips/Lavinsky), and personal sites (mellowood.ca, lcurtisboyle.com).

summary: Promote rng/rnd to kernel primitives
description: Move the RNG from forth/lib/rng.fs into kernel.asm. Add VAR_SEED kernel variable, a CODE_RNG primitive ( -- ) that advances seed = seed*25173+13849, and a CODE_RND primitive ( n -- 0..n-1 ) for power-of-two ranges. Expose via fc.py kernel_words() as 'rng' and 'rnd', plus KVAR-SEED for direct access. Drop rng.fs (or keep the file as a thin compatibility shim). Estimated ~25 bytes of kernel. Benefits: every app gets a fast RNG without INCLUDE; demos (Tetris, Rain, Kaleidoscope) save Forth dispatch overhead on every random call.

summary: FujiNet support: lib/fujinet.fs with RTC (FN-TIME)
description: Add a Forth library that talks to the CoCo FujiNet cartridge. Scope this issue: dw-write / dw-read CODE words that wrap the FujiNet cart's bit-banger ROM vectors at $F9FE / $FA00 (mirroring the calling convention used by fujinet-lib/coco/src/bus/dwread.c and dwwrite.c — X=buffer, Y=length, carry-out=success). Plus fn-ready (DriveWire $E2,$00 ping handshake) and fn-time (sends $23, reads 6 bytes [year-1900, mm, dd, hh, mm, ss]). CODE words must save the kernel S register (which lives at $DFxx, inside the ROM-mapped overlay) and switch to a temp stack via PC-relative addressing before flipping SAM TY to ROM mode (STA $FFDE) for the JSR, then restore all-RAM (STA $FFDF) and S after. This is the same stack-relocation pattern documented in src/sound/SOUND.md — sound's audio path is broken for unrelated PIA reasons but the call/return mechanism is proven. Demo: src/fujinet-time/fujinet-time.fs displays YYYY.MM.DD HH:MM:SS on the VDG screen. Future issues will add network/disk APIs.

summary: Demo: future-retro analog+digital clock backed by FujiNet RTC
description: src/clock/clock.fs — showcase demo for the FujiNet RTC library. RG6 (256x192, 3-color artifact palette). Composition: large centered analog face (~120px diameter) with white circle outline, 12 white tick marks (longer at 12/3/6/9), no numerals; hour hand (thick white, ~1/3 radius), minute hand (thin white, ~2/3 radius), second hand (thin red, ~3/4 radius) sweeping smoothly at vsync rate; small filled white center pin. Digital block below: HH:MM:SS with red colon that pulses on the second, date YYYY.MM.DD on the line above. Small 'FUJI' badge in a corner with a red dot that flashes briefly on each successful FN sync. Time source: query fn-time once at boot, then advance locally via 60Hz vsync counter (NTSC is actually 59.94Hz, ~1s drift per 17min). Resync every ~5min (configurable) absorbs drift. Animation: beam.fs trace/draw/restore for flicker-free hand redraw — no full double buffer needed. Math: trig.fs for sin/cos. Strokes: rg-pixel.fs (rg-line). Text: rg-text.fs / font-art.fs. Hand angles from 12: hour=(h*30+m/2), minute=(m*6+s/10), second=(s*6+vsync_in_sec/10).

summary: Clock: double-buffer to eliminate hand-tick flicker
description: src/clock/clock.fs currently does erase-all + redraw-all-hands inline on the visible VRAM, which spans more than one vblank window and produces a brief flicker each second. Switch to true double-buffering: allocate a second 6KB RG6 VRAM at $A000-$B7FF (clear of font at $9000 and kernel at $E000), keep both buffers initialised with face + ticks + pin + label chars, then per second-tick render new hands and digital readout to the back buffer and flip SAM-F at vsync. Each VRAM needs its own set of 3 beam-path buffers (SC/MN/HR) since each buffer's previous hand state is independent. Display stays at 60fps even though the render itself can span 2-3 frames; flip is instant. Cost: ~6KB extra RAM (have it), ~150 bytes extra beam state, more complex init. End result: zero visible flicker on tick.

summary: Clock: sub-vsync precision for vps calibration
description: src/clock/clock.fs adaptive vsync calibration uses 16-bit integer vps, which truncates the fractional part of vps-cnt/real-sec. That leaves up to ~0.5 sec/min residual drift between FN syncs. Fix: scale vps and vs-cnt by 16 (4 fractional bits). INITIAL-VPS becomes 960. tick-frame increments vs-cnt by 16 instead of 1. New helper scaled-div computes (a*16)/b safely without 16-bit overflow by splitting a into quot+rem first (vps-cnt up to ~4000 would overflow if directly multiplied by 16). EWMA blending unchanged (just operates at higher precision). Drops residual drift to ~0.03 sec/min — invisible.

summary: Audit + polish all demos to be exceptional and bullet-proof
description: These demos are showcase examples of bare-naked Forth as a development environment for the CoCo Renovation project. They should be exceptional. Audit each src/* demo and file specific sub-issues for any defect, rough edge, or missed polish. Demos in scope: bounce, calculator, clock, fujinet-time, kaleidoscope, rain, rg-test, sound (broken-known), tetris, typewriter, vdg-modes. Audit dimensions: (1) timing/sync correctness (no drift, no flicker, no tearing); (2) input handling (debounce, edge cases, key release); (3) graceful error handling (no crashes on missing devices like FN); (4) visual quality (artifact-color hygiene, composition, overlap-free animation); (5) clean exits (HALT vs spin); (6) documentation header in each .fs (purpose, build, load, controls). Each defect gets a distinct sub-issue.

summary: bounce: missing Build/Load documentation header
description:
bounce.fs describes Controls but lacks Build: and Load: documentation. Users won't know how to compile and run it. Add standard header:

Build:   make
Load:    LOADM"BOUNCE":EXEC

summary: bounce: infinite AGAIN loop without exit sequence
description: bounce.fs line 212 uses BEGIN...AGAIN with no exit condition. The demo runs forever with no way to cleanly exit. Should add a quit key (e.g. BREAK) or document that users must close XRoar / power-cycle the CoCo.

summary: bounce: doesn't restore text mode on exit
description: bounce.fs switches to RG mode (rg-init at line 92) but never calls reset-text before exiting. If the program halts, the screen stays in RG mode instead of VDG text. Should restore VDG text mode for a clean exit.

summary: calculator: missing Build/Load documentation header
description: calc.fs has no Build: or Load: instructions at the top. Add standard header.

summary: calculator: FLASH-AT busy-wait uses hardcoded iteration count
description: calc.fs line 239: `2000 0 DO LOOP` is a hardcoded delay loop (~2ms on ~0.9 MHz CPU). Timing-dependent and non-portable across CoCo speeds. Better: count VSYNC frames or use a kernel-provided delay primitive.

summary: kaleidoscope: missing Build/Load documentation header
description: kaleidoscope.fs has no Build: or Load: instructions. Add standard header.

summary: kaleidoscope: infinite loop with no exit path
description: kaleidoscope.fs line 124 uses 0 UNTIL (infinite loop). No quit key or exit condition. Same pattern as bounce/rain. Add a quit key or document explicitly.

summary: kaleidoscope: doesn't restore text mode on exit
description: kaleidoscope.fs calls set-sam-f and set-sam-v to switch video modes but never restores VDG text. Screen stays in graphics mode if the program halts. Should call reset-text before HALT.

summary: rain: missing Build/Load documentation header
description: rain.fs lacks Build: and Load: instructions. Add standard header.

summary: rain: infinite loop with no exit path
description: rain.fs line 192 uses 0 UNTIL (infinite). No quit key. Add a quit key or document explicitly.

summary: rg-test: missing Build/Load documentation header
description: rg-test.fs lacks Build: and Load: instructions. Add standard header.

summary: rg-test: blocking KEY at end can hang on stuck-key edge case
description: rg-test.fs line 164: `KEY DROP` between tests is fine for interactive use but blocks on undefined keys. Consider `KEY?` polling with a fallback timeout for batch / automated testing scenarios.

summary: sound: stub references non-existent SND_PLAY symbol
description: src/sound/sound.fs CODE word JSRs SND_PLAY which is not defined in the kernel. Demo cannot build cleanly today. Either remove the demo (preferred until sound works) or restore as a clearly-marked EXPERIMENTAL placeholder. Audio path is broken at PIA level under all-RAM mode — see src/sound/SOUND.md for full investigation notes.

summary: tetris: missing Build/Load documentation header
description: tetris.fs has excellent doc but lacks Build: and Load: instructions at the top. Add standard header.

summary: typewriter: missing Build/Load documentation header
description: typewriter.fs describes key behavior but lacks Build: and Load: instructions. Add standard header.

summary: typewriter: clarify exit behavior in doc header
description: typewriter.fs uses BEGIN/AGAIN with the only exit being BREAK key ($03) calling 'halt' (line 94). The exit path works but is undocumented in the file's header — readers need to scan to find it. Add a 'Press BREAK to exit' line to the doc header.

summary: vdg-modes: missing Build/Load documentation header
description: vdg-modes.fs lacks Build: and Load: instructions. Add standard header.

summary: vdg-modes: doesn't restore text mode on exit
description: vdg-modes.fs cycles through 11 display modes but line 228 calls HALT without reset-text. Screen stays in whatever mode was last shown. Should restore VDG text for a clean exit.

summary: fujinet-time: standardize Build/Load header format
description: fujinet-time.fs has Build:/Load: instructions inline in the header (lines 7-8) but not in the same format other demos use. Once all demos adopt a standard header (issues 421/424/426/429/431/434/435/437), align this one too.

summary: fujinet.fs: fn-ready spins forever when no FN attached
description: forth/lib/fujinet.fs fn-ready calls fn-ping in a UNTIL loop with no timeout. If no FujiNet is present (or DW server isn't responding), apps that call fn-ready or fn-time at boot hang indefinitely with no visual feedback. Add a fn-ready/N variant with a max retry count and a fail flag, or document that callers should poll fn-ping themselves with their own timeout. Currently the clock demo and any future FN demo will hang at boot if FN absent.

summary: clock: graceful fallback when FujiNet absent
description: src/clock/clock.fs calls sync-from-fn at boot. If FN isn't reachable the demo hangs forever inside fn-ready (depends on issue 440). Even if a fn-ready timeout exists, the clock should DECIDE what to do on failure — best options: (a) fall back to a placeholder time and show 'NO RTC' badge, (b) display an error screen, (c) retry every N seconds in the background. Without one of these, removing the FN cart bricks the demo.

summary: Top-level Makefile dsk target missing newer demos
description: Makefile DEMOS variable lists bounce, calculator, kaleidoscope, rain, tetris, rg-test, typewriter — but src/ also contains clock, fujinet-time, sound, vdg-modes. `make dsk` therefore omits 4 demos from build/demos.dsk. Either add them to DEMOS (and to the dsk decb-copy block) or document why they're excluded.

summary: kernel.asm: stale comment on START says 'copied to $8000'
description: forth/kernel/kernel.asm line 1505 comment reads 'Called by BOOTSTRAP after the kernel has been copied to $8000.' That was true before commit 2a53d5f relocated the kernel from $8000 to $E000. Update to '$E000'. Pure docfix, no behavior change.

summary: Per-demo Makefiles are near-identical copy-paste
description: Each src/<demo>/Makefile is ~25 lines of almost the same content (KERNEL_DIR, FC, XROAR_ROMS, all/run/kernel/clean targets). When a single shared default changes (e.g. xroar args, kernel path), we have to edit 11 files. Refactor to a shared include — e.g. make/demo.mk that each demo's Makefile sources via `include ../../make/demo.mk`, with just a NAME var per demo. Reduces churn and copy-paste drift.

summary: Clock: smooth (Rolex-style) second-hand sweep at 60Hz instead of per-second tick
description: src/clock/clock.fs currently redraws the second hand only when clk-sc changes (1 Hz), so it jumps from one second mark to the next. Make it sweep continuously: redraw on every vsync (60 Hz), advancing the angle by sub-second amounts derived from vs-cnt/vps. Per-frame cost ~5500 cycles is well under one frame; min/hr only redrawn on minute change (mn-pending counter spreads the heavier tick-hands work across the next two back buffers). Keep digital readout updates at 1 Hz inside the existing sec-change IF block.

summary: KERNEL: beam-trace BEQ tests wrong flag, corrupts saved color for x%4==3 pixels
description:
forth/kernel/kernel.asm CODE_BEAM_TRACE around line 2063-2068 does:

  LDB ,Y       ; Z set based on VRAM byte
  PSHS A
  BEQ @nr      ; meant to skip shift when A==0 but tests B's Z
  @rsh LSRB / DECA / BNE @rsh
  @nr ANDB #$03

When shift amount A==0 (x%4==3) and the VRAM byte is non-zero, BEQ does NOT branch, enters the shift loop. DECA wraps A from 0 to 255, shifts B right 255+ times, B becomes 0. Saved color is 0 regardless of actual pixel color.

Result: 25% of pixels along any beam-traced line have corrupted saved color. beam-restore then paints those pixels black instead of restoring original color. Masked in demos that redraw before accumulated damage is visible, but exposed by the smooth-sweep clock where the minute hand stays on-screen for 60s of sec-hand passes. Minute hand visibly becomes dashed/chewed.

Fix: add TSTA before BEQ to test A instead of B's leftover Z flag. One-line change.

summary: Clock perf: angle-dx / angle-dy as CODE words
description: forth/lib/trig.fs angle-dx and angle-dy are each ~2,824 cycles per call (DOCOL + cos/sin 7-bit lookup + Forth * + signed /128 + EXIT). Each hand redraw via ep1/ep2 calls them twice (once for dx, once for dy), so redraw-sc-back spends ~5,500 of its 8,396 cycles in these two words. Converting to 6809 CODE with inline sin table lookup + MUL + ASR would drop each to ~400-500cy. Expected savings: typical clock frame from 9,608cy (64% budget) to ~4,500cy (30%); sec-change frame from 18,562cy (124%) to ~13,500cy (under budget). Largest single optimization available for clock smoothness.

summary: Clock perf: selective render-datetime — only update changed digits
description: src/clock/clock.fs render-datetime re-draws all 16 characters (YYYY.MM.DD and HH:MM:SS) each sec change, costing ~7,174cy. The only values that change every second are the 2 ss digits. Split render-datetime into render-date (re-draw only on dy/mo/yr change), render-hm (only on mn change), and render-ss (every sec). Saves ~6,000cy on 58/60 dig-pending frames per second once we pass the initial paint. Easy optimization; low risk. Done: split into render-date (4,301cy, day/boot only), render-hm (1,961cy, minute/boot only), and render-ss (912cy, every sec change). Replaced dig-pending with three counters (ss-pending, hm-pending, date-pending). Sec-change add-on drops from 8,954cy to 2,692cy — whole frame now comfortably under budget.

summary: Clock perf: skip render-sync-flash when no flash pending
description: src/clock/clock.fs render-sync-flash renders 8 characters (either 'RTC SYNC' or 8 spaces to clear) on every dig-pending frame. After the initial 2-frame flash clears on both buffers, we keep re-rendering spaces every dig-pending tick until the next flash fires. Gate render-sync-flash with a flash-dirty-pending counter so it only runs for 2 frames after the flash counter changes state. Minor savings (~1,780cy) but tidier.

summary: Clock perf: tick-hands as CODE word
description: src/clock/clock.fs tick-hands costs ~24,558cy (erase 3 hands + redraw all 3 in z-order).  Fires on mn-pending for 2 frames per minute.  Each firing takes ~3 real frames, losing 4 real frames per minute.  Converting to CODE with inlined ep/trace/paint logic could cut to ~8,000cy, making it fit in 1 real frame.  Lower priority than #447 since it only fires 2x/min.

summary: Clock perf: precompute sin table as kernel data
description: forth/lib/trig.fs init-sin builds the 91-entry sin table by writing values one at a time via the tb helper (cost: 25,444cy one-time at boot).  Could be embedded as raw FCB data in the kernel (like font-data, sprite-data), copied once via CMOVE — same pattern as #162 (font) and #163 (sprite data).  Costs 91 bytes of kernel space, saves ~25,000cy of boot time and significant app binary size.  Paired benefit with #447 if both move to CODE.

summary: Clock perf: precomputed sec-hand endpoint tables
description: The smooth-sweep second hand redraws every frame and calls ep2 (~5,917cy) which internally calls angle-dx and angle-dy. Since the sec-hand radius is fixed (R-SC=46), precompute two 360-byte tables — sec-tx-tab[angle] holds (CX + sin(angle)*R-SC/2) and sec-ty-tab[angle] holds (CY - cos(angle)*R-SC) — and index them directly in redraw-sc-back. Eliminates ep2, clk>trig, and angle-dx/dy calls from the per-frame path. Expected savings: ~5,700cy per frame. Makes the loop fit comfortably within one real vblank in fake-time mode, so local seconds no longer drift. 720 bytes of binary cost. Narrower scope than issue #447 but solves the drift observed in fake-time. Verified in XRoar fake-time: drift dropped from 2.17x slow to 1.07x slow (120 real-sec = 110 fake-sec). Combined table lookup + pixel-endpoint dedup — most frames now do zero pixel work since consecutive angles share an integer tx/ty.

summary: Clock: smooth-sweep for hour and minute hands too
description: The sec hand glides; hr and mn jerk forward once per minute/hour. Goal: all three hands advance continuously, proportional to wall time, so the clock flows instead of ticks. Implementation: precompute endpoint tables for hr (R=24) and mn (R=40) just like the existing sec tables (#452). Replace discrete mn-angle/hr-angle with smooth variants derived from sc-angle (mn-angle-smooth = clk-mn*6 + sc-angle/60; hr-angle-smooth = clk-hr*30 + mn-angle-smooth/12). Each buffer tracks last-rendered endpoints for all three hands. Every frame: compute current endpoints via table lookup, find the lowest hand whose endpoint changed (hr < mn < sc by z-order), erase top-down to that hand, redraw bottom-up from that hand. Adds two pairs of 360-byte tables (1,440 extra bytes) and extends flip-state to swap six more pairs (hr-ltx/lty, mn-ltx/lty). Visible benefit: the hour hand drifts to the next integer degree every ~2 minutes; minute hand every ~10 seconds; all completely smoothly. No more visible tick events. Done: added 4 new endpoint tables (hr-tx-tab, hr-ty-tab, mn-tx-tab, mn-ty-tab — 1,440 bytes). Added 6 new lasts (hr/mn ltx/lty, both buffers) and extended flip-state to swap them. New unified redraw-hands replaces the old redraw-sc-back/tick-hands split: every frame computes endpoints, finds lowest moved hand by z-order, erases top-down and redraws bottom-up. Verified in XRoar fake-time: 67 real-sec = 60 fake-sec (1.08x slow — same as before despite the smoother motion, because the dedup absorbs the extra cost). All three hands visible and tracking correctly.

summary: Clock cosmetics: pip ticks for non-quarter positions + 10%-wider face
description: Two visual improvements to the analog face. (1) The diagonal 2-pixel tick marks at 1, 2, 4, 5, 7, 8, 10, 11 o'clock look approximate at low resolution — replace with a 2×2 pixel square (pip) at the outer edge so they read cleanly. The 12, 3, 6, 9 quarter marks stay as longer line segments. (2) Stretch the entire clock geometry (face + ticks + all 3 hands) horizontally by 10% so it better matches the CRT's wider-than-tall aspect ratio. Implementation: change the ep1/ep2 X-displacement compensation from /2 to ×11/20 (= 0.55), regenerate the three endpoint tables (sec/mn/hr) with the same 0.55 X-scale, replace the diagonal-tick branch in face-ticks with a 2×2 rg-pset block. Done. Added x-scale helper that handles signed-multiply via NEGATE-around (kernel * and / are unsigned, so naive 11 * 20 / wraps for negative dx). Regenerated all three hand endpoint tables (sec/mn/hr) with X scale 0.55. Replaced the diagonal-tick branch in face-ticks with a 2x2 rg-pset block. Verified in XRoar: face is visibly wider matching CRT aspect, 4 quarter ticks remain as line segments, 8 hour pips render cleanly.

summary: Clock perf: angle words (sc/mn/hr-angle) to CODE
description: redraw-hands base cost is ~5,560cy/frame, of which ~3,940cy is the three angle calculations (sc-angle 751, mn-angle 1,268, hr-angle 1,924). All three are simple integer math: sc = clk-sc*6 + (vs-cnt*6)/vps, mn = clk-mn*6 + sc/60, hr = (clk-hr%12)*30 + mn/12. The dominant cost is the 16-bit divide (Forth's /MOD is repeated subtract). CODE versions can use 6809 MUL for the *6 multiplications and shift-subtract for the divides (quotients are small: 0-5, 0-5, 0-29). Expected: ~3,000cy/frame saved, baseline drops from 6,876cy to ~3,876cy (26% budget). Brings minute-boundary frames under budget. Done. Added compute-angles CODE word (282cy) that computes all three angles in one pass using 6809 MUL + repeated-subtract for the three divides. redraw-hands and tick-hands now call it and read from new sa/ma/ha cached cells. Measured savings: baseline frame 6,876 → 3,216cy; redraw-hands worst case 11,732 → 8,251cy. Every frame now fits in one vblank except the once-per-day midnight overrun. Minute-boundary frames (previously 120% over budget) now at 96%.

summary: Clock perf: redraw-hands as a single CODE word
description: Once #455 lands, the remaining ~2,000cy of base overhead in redraw-hands lives in the table lookups (6 of them), the cascade compares (3), and the lasts updates (6). Inlining the whole word in 6809 asm — register-driven cascade dispatch, direct memory addressing for the FVAR_*_lt[xy] cells, JSR to existing beam-trace/draw/restore CODE primitives — would eliminate that overhead. Estimated: another ~2,000cy/frame saved, baseline drops from ~3,876 to ~1,876cy (13% budget). Big-asm change (~150 lines), only worth doing after #455.
